subject: Product Management
title: The Lean Startup Method
description: A 60-second lesson on The Lean Startup Method.

hook.text: Eric Ries was burning through cash building features nobody wanted.

buildup.text: The old model: spend months building a product, launch it, and hope customers come.

discovery.text: A Minimum Viable Product (MVP) isn't a bad product. It's the smallest thing that tests your riskiest hypothesis.

twist.text: The hardest part of Lean isn't building MVPs — it's accepting the results. When data says your idea doesn't work, ego resists.

climax.text: The Lean Startup transformed product development from a faith-based activity into a scientific one.

punchline.text: Build the smallest thing that tests the biggest risk. Learn fast.

quiz.question: What is the core principle of the Lean Startup method?
options: Test the riskiest assumption first with the smallest possible product — Build, Measure, Learn
Build the complete product before testing anything
The Lean method eliminates the need for experimentation
